
Two arXiv papers from the RSS scout stand out as strong candidates. 'HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning' directly addresses agentic workflow architecture with self-evolution and hybrid design patterns. 'Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use' covers orchestration and tool-use design with empirical grounding. The Grok signals are mostly framework release announcements or existing awesome-lists that don't meet the bar for this curated list. The other arXiv papers are too narrowly focused on planning SAT encodings, sketching, or RL without agentic workflow design focus.

## Suggestions

- [HyEvo: Self-Evolving Hybrid Agentic Workflows for Efficient Reasoning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19639) — Proposes a self-evolving hybrid agentic workflow architecture that dynamically adapts workflow structure and tool-use patterns during reasoning, with empirical evaluation on reasoning benchmarks.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Iterative refinement practices (evaluation loops, human-in-the-loop, observability), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)

- [Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.19896) — Presents a utility-guided orchestration framework for selecting and sequencing LLM tool calls efficiently, with empirical benchmarks demonstrating reduced cost and improved task completion.
  Section: Research Papers | Criteria: Architectural guidance for goal-directed agent systems (patterns, orchestration, state management), Tool-use and execution design across agent lifecycle stages (planning, execution, feedback, recovery), Empirical or practical grounding (case studies, benchmarks, production deployment evidence)
